surface.name: Stripe-hosted Checkout via cURL Checkout Session
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Complete a Stripe-hosted Checkout payment. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: A cURL-created Checkout Session opens on Stripe's hosted page and completes a test-mode payment.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Stripe setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A cURL-created Checkout Session opens on Stripe's hosted page and completes a test-mode payment.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Stripe-hosted Checkout displays the successful completion flow after test card details are submitted.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Complete a Stripe-hosted Checkout payment.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Stripe-hosted Checkout via cURL Checkout Session surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: A Stripe account with test mode available.
A test-mode Price ID for the Checkout line item.
A terminal with cURL and a browser.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Stripe and switch to test mode.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.output: Test-mode Developers and product settings are accessible.
primary_path.success_signal: Test-mode Developers and product settings are accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Create or select a test product price and copy its Price ID and test secret key.
primary_path.output: A test Price ID and secret key are available.
primary_path.success_signal: A test Price ID and secret key are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Use cURL to create a Checkout Session with the Price ID, success URL, cancel URL, and test secret key.
primary_path.output: Stripe returns a Checkout Session with a hosted URL.
primary_path.success_signal: Stripe returns a Checkout Session with a hosted URL.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Open the returned Checkout URL and submit Stripe's documented test payment details.
primary_path.output: Stripe accepts the test payment and completes Checkout.
primary_path.success_signal: Stripe accepts the test payment and completes Checkout.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Confirm the hosted completion flow or configured success redirect is reached.
primary_path.output: The test Checkout payment is complete.
primary_path.success_signal: The test Checkout payment is complete.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: The secret key and Price ID must both be from test mode.
Checkout Session creation requires valid success and cancel URLs for the selected mode.

sources.title: Build a Stripe-hosted checkout page
evidence_supported: A cURL-created Checkout Session opens on Stripe's hosted page and completes a test-mode payment.
Stripe-hosted Checkout displays the successful completion flow after test card details are submitted.

sources.title: Create a Checkout Session
evidence_supported: A cURL-created Checkout Session opens on Stripe's hosted page and completes a test-mode payment.
Stripe-hosted Checkout displays the successful completion flow after test card details are submitted.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Complete a Stripe-hosted Checkout payment path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
